title: Toast
method: Toast bread until golden brown.
Spread butter immediately while hot so it melts in.

title: Steamed Rice
method: Rinse rice until water runs clear.
Add to pot with water and salt, bring to a boil.
Cover and simmer on the lowest heat for 12 minutes.
Rest off heat for 5 minutes before fluffing.

title: Baked Chips
method: Preheat oven to 220°C.
Cut potatoes into wedges, toss with oil and salt.
Spread on a baking tray in a single layer.
Bake 35-40 minutes, turning halfway, until golden and crisp.

title: Orange Pasta
method: Dice mushrooms, garlic, and onion.
Fry onion and mince.
Add mushroom and diced garlic, don't fry too long.
Add tomatoes, cream, and beef stock.
Combine well and add pasta.
Lid on or add water to keep it wet — soak up and boil off most of the liquid.

title: Fried Rice
method: Heat a wok or large pan on high until very hot.
Add oil and fry any raw extras (e.g. mushrooms) until cooked. Set aside.
Add a little more oil, crack in the eggs, scramble until just set then break into chunks.
Add the rice, breaking up any clumps. Stir-fry on high heat for 2-3 minutes.
Add ham and cooked extras back in. Toss everything together.
Season with soy sauce to taste. Serve immediately.

title: Omelette
ingredients: Fillings, diced small (onion, mushroom, ham, cheese, whatever's there)
method: Dice fillings small. Fry in oil until cooked. Set aside in a bowl.
Crack eggs into a jug or bowl, season, and beat well. Add the fried fillings and stir until everything is wet.
Heat pan on medium-high with a little oil or butter.
Pour in the egg mixture and spread it out. Let it set on the bottom.
One flip when the top is mostly set. Cook briefly on the other side.
Slide out and serve with toast.
